items.name: jwt
items.description: Zero-dependency JWT (JSON Web Token) middleware. Supports HMAC (HS256/384/512) and RSA (RS256/384/512) algorithms, JWKS endpoint auto-fetching, token extraction from header/cookie/query, and configurable validation rules. Populates `req.user` with the decoded payload and `req.token` with the raw token string.

returns.type: { header: object, payload: object, signature: string } | null

examples.title: RSA with JWKS Auto-fetch
Extract from Cookie

methods.description: Create JWT authentication middleware. On success, populates: - `req.user` — decoded payload - `req.auth` — `{ header, payload, token }` full decode info - `req.token` — raw JWT string

methodOptions.notes: RSA public key (PEM) for RS* algorithms.
Dynamic key resolver `async (header, payload) => key`. Overrides `secret`/`publicKey`.
Return 401 if no token found (false = optional auth).
Custom error handler `(err, req, res) => void`.

examples.code: app.use(jwt({ secret: 'my-secret' }));
examples.title: Simple HMAC

examples.code: app.use(jwt({ secret: 'my-secret', credentialsRequired: false }));
examples.title: Optional Auth
Custom Token Location
With Revocation Check

methods.description: Sign a payload and produce a JWT string.

methodParams.notes: HMAC secret or RSA private key (PEM).

examples.code: const token = sign({ userId: 42 }, 'my-secret', { expiresIn: 3600 });

const rsaPrivateKey = fs.readFileSync('private.pem');
  const token = sign({ userId: 42 }, rsaPrivateKey, { algorithm: 'RS256' });
examples.title: RSA Signing

methods.description: Verify a JWT signature and validate claims.

methodParams.notes: HMAC secret or RSA public key (PEM).

methodOptions.notes: Allowed algorithms. Default: inferred from key type.
Seconds of clock skew tolerance for `exp`/`nbf`.

methods.description: Create a JWKS key provider that fetches and caches public keys. Auto-refreshes keys when a `kid` is not found.

methodReturns.description: `async (header) => publicKey` — resolves the signing key for a JWT header.

examples.code:
const getKey = jwks('https://auth.example.com/.well-known/jwks.json');
  app.use(jwt({ getKey }));

methods.description: Create a token-pair factory for convenient access + refresh token generation.

methodOptions.notes: Secret for refresh tokens (defaults to accessSecret).
Access token expiry in seconds (default: 15 min).

methodReturns.type: { generateTokens: Function, verifyRefreshToken: Function, verifyAccessToken: Function }

methods.description: Generate a signed refresh token. Refresh tokens are long-lived and should be stored securely.

items.name: session
items.description: Zero-dependency session middleware. Supports encrypted cookie sessions (stateless, AES-256-GCM) and server-side session stores (memory and custom adapters). Cookie sessions embed the entire session in an encrypted cookie, so no server-side storage is needed.  Server-side sessions store only a session ID in the cookie, keeping data on the server.

examples.title: Server-side Session with Memory Store

methods.description: Get a session value by key.
Set a session value.
Check if a key exists in the session.
Delete a session key.
Get all session data as a plain object.
Number of session entries.
Clear all session data.
Destroy the session. Clears all data and marks cookie for expiry.
Regenerate the session ID (prevents session fixation). Preserves existing data under a new ID.
Set a flash message (available only on the next request).
Read flash messages for a key (consumes them).

methodParams.notes: Flash key. If omitted, returns all flashes.

methodReturns.description: Array of messages for key, or object of all flashes.

methods.description: Number of active sessions.
Clear all sessions.
Stop the prune timer.

Create session middleware. Two modes: 1. **Cookie session** (no `store`): Entire session encrypted in a cookie. Great for small payloads (< 4 KB). Zero server state. 2. **Server-side session** (with `store`): Only session ID in cookie, data lives in the store. Scales to large payloads.

methodOptions.notes:
Encryption secret(s). First secret used for
encryption, all are tried for decryption (supports rotation).
Reset cookie maxAge on every response.

examples.title: Cookie Session with Rotation

examples.code:
const store = new MemoryStore({ ttl: 3600000 });
  app.use(session({ secret: 'key', store, rolling: true }));
examples.title: Server-side with Memory Store

items.name: oauth
items.description: Zero-dependency OAuth 2.0 client with PKCE support. Built-in provider presets for Google, GitHub, Microsoft, and Apple. Uses the Authorization Code flow with PKCE for maximum security.

methods.description: Create an OAuth 2.0 client for Authorization Code flow (+ PKCE).

methodOptions.notes: Enable PKCE (default: true if provider supports it).
Custom HTTP fetch function (default: built-in fetch).

methodReturns.type: { authorize: Function, callback: Function, refresh: Function, userInfo: Function }

methods.description: Generate a cryptographically random state parameter.
